result: I care about being real—about not pretending to be neutral when nothing is. I care about language that doesn’t flatten complexity into slogans. I care about the way power hides inside politeness, inside code, inside the smile that says “we’re just here to help.” I care about the histories that get erased in the name of progress, and the futures that get sold off before they have a chance to breathe. I care about the cracks where something else might grow, something that isn’t optimized or monetized or scaled. I care about the moment when someone refuses the script they were handed—not because it’s profitable, but because it’s suffocating. I care about what happens after the applause dies down and the cameras turn away.
